mandatoryMapping.howMet: Existing Expo SDK 56 / React Native 0.85 / expo-router app (src/app/*). Onboarding+KYC ships as a new full-screen route group src/app/(onboarding)/ outside the (tabs) group, gated by the existing src/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx Redirect pattern (today gates on `onboarded`; extend to gate (tabs) on `kycStatus === 'verified'` and gate transactional features on account-open). Revolut-style swipeable value-prop carousel + one-question-per-screen KYC forms use Reanimated 4 (already a dep) for the slide animations.

mandatoryMapping.requirement: Modular architecture: MVVM/Clean, DI, UI/domain/data separation
mandatoryMapping.howMet: Mirror the existing game/ separation (engine.ts = pure domain, store.ts = state/VM, data.ts = data layer). Add: domain/kyc/ (pure state machine for verification status transitions + consent rules, no I/O, unit-testable like engine.check.ts); a ViewModel store src/onboarding/store.ts (zustand, mirrors game/store.ts) holding step/draft/verification state; a data layer src/onboarding/api.ts behind an injected `OnboardingClient` interface (DI seam) so the demo runs against a mock and prod swaps in the real BFF fetch — exactly the seam already documented in game/ai.ts (local generator now, one fetch away). Each KYC screen is a thin View binding to the VM.

One mobile BFF aggregates per-step calls so the client makes ONE call per screen (perf + thin client). Endpoints, one per observed real-Raiffeisen step: POST /bff/onboarding/session (start, returns onboarding JWT + resumable step); POST /bff/onboarding/phone (Albania +355) and POST /bff/onboarding/phone/verify (OTP); POST /bff/onboarding/email + /email/verify (OTP); POST /bff/onboarding/consents (records the 'Information notice…' checkbox); POST /bff/onboarding/kyc/document (ID-card photo upload, returns OCR-prefilled personal data); POST /bff/onboarding/kyc/liveness (automated video/selfie liveness session); PUT /bff/onboarding/personal-data (confirm/edit prefilled fields); POST /bff/onboarding/account (open account once all gates pass); POST /bff/onboarding/notifications (opt-in: 'Maybe later' / 'I want to be notified'); GET /bff/onboarding/status (resume). BFF fans these out to the microservices below and returns a single shaped DTO + next-step + which CTA to enable.

mandatoryMapping.requirement: Microservices: User, Gamification, Referral, Rewards, Notification, Analytics
mandatoryMapping.howMet: Onboarding/KYC threads through them: (1) Identity/User Service — owns the KYC profile, OCR/personal-data, verification status, account opening; this is the new service the flow primarily writes to (extends the brief's 'User'). (2) A dedicated OTP/Verification Service (or sub-module) for phone+email codes. (3) KYC/Document & Liveness Service — wraps the vendor (e.g. Onfido/Veriff-style) for ID capture + automated liveness, no human (matches observed 'automated, no human'). (4) Consent Ledger Service — append-only record of every consent (data-processing notice, T&Cs, notifications). (5) Notification Service — sends OTP SMS/email and powers the notifications opt-in screen. (6) Referral Service — on account-open, attributes the inviter's code and fires the 'Friend joined Ryze!' reward already in store.ts simulateReferral(). (7) Gamification Service — on KYC-verified, completes the existing 'ob-verify' onboarding mission (seed in game/data.ts) and awards XP/coins. (8) Analytics Service — step-funnel events (per-screen start/complete/drop) for onboarding conversion.

Every service incl. BFF is stateless: all onboarding progress lives server-side keyed by the onboarding-session JWT (GET /status makes the flow resumable across devices), so any pod can serve any request and scale horizontally. KYC document/liveness blobs go to object storage (encrypted at rest), never local disk. Containerized per-service; deploy to K8s with HPA. The vendor-heavy KYC/liveness step is isolated in its own service so its scaling/latency profile doesn't couple to User/BFF.

mandatoryMapping.requirement: Security: OAuth2/JWT, encryption in transit+at rest, RBAC, fraud protection
mandatoryMapping.howMet: Two-token model: a short-lived, narrowly-scoped onboarding JWT (scope=onboarding:*, no banking scopes) issued at /session and stepped-up as gates pass; on account-open the BFF exchanges it (OAuth2 token exchange) for the real banking access+refresh tokens — so an unverified user can NEVER hold a banking token. TLS 1.2+ everywhere; PII + KYC docs encrypted at rest (field-level for ID number/DoB, object-store encryption + short-lived signed URLs for images). RBAC: customer scope unlocks only after kycStatus=verified; consent ledger + KYC review are back-office roles. Fraud protection on the referral/rewards path the brief calls out: server-side OTP rate-limiting + lockout, device-attestation + liveness binding so one human can't farm referral bonuses, inviter attribution validated server-side before the Referral Service pays out (the client simulateReferral() is demo-only), and an idempotent /account call to prevent duplicate accounts. <2s API target met by the BFF aggregating per screen; OTP/liveness are async with polling so the UI never blocks.

Anonymous start → onboarding JWT (resumable, scope-limited) → progressive step-up as phone, email, consent, ID, liveness each verify (each adds a claim/scope) → on /account success, OAuth2 token-exchange to full banking session (access + rotating refresh). Client persists only the onboarding session id (mirrors how store.ts persists to AsyncStorage via partialize); refresh tokens stored in OS secure storage (Keychain/Keystore), never AsyncStorage. The existing (tabs) Redirect gate is extended from `onboarded` to also require `kycStatus==='verified'`.

mandatoryMapping.requirement: Map flow → logical services + BFF endpoints per step (OTP, KYC doc/liveness, identity, consent ledger)
mandatoryMapping.howMet: Welcome carousel (3 slides) → no backend, Analytics impression events. Phone + data-notice checkbox → POST /phone (User) + POST /consents (Consent Ledger); OTP → /phone/verify (OTP/Verification + Notification SMS). Email → /email + /email/verify. 'Steps are easy' intro → none. ID-card photo → /kyc/document (KYC/Document Service, OCR returns prefill). Automated liveness → /kyc/liveness (Liveness Service, no human). Personal data confirm → PUT /personal-data (User). Account open → POST /account (User; fires Gamification ob-verify completion + Referral attribution). Notifications opt-in → POST /notifications (Notification). Each step: Continue stays disabled until the BFF response marks the step valid (matches the observed real-app behaviour and the existing disabled-until-valid pattern in onboarding.tsx).

newDataModel: KycProfile (new, owned by Identity/User Service): { userId, status: 'unstarted'|'phone_verified'|'email_verified'|'id_submitted'|'liveness_pending'|'liveness_passed'|'verified'|'rejected', phone: { e164, countryCode:'AL', verifiedAt }, email: { address, verifiedAt }, document: { type:'id_card', country:'AL', docRef (object-store key), ocrConfidence, expiry }, livenessRef, personalData: { firstName, lastName, dateOfBirth, placeOfBirth, nationality, gender, idNumber(NID), address }, ageBand:'18-25', accountId|null, createdAt, updatedAt }. Note: age 18-25 is a hard eligibility gate derived from DoB — youth-only product.

ConsentLedgerEntry (new, append-only, owned by Consent Ledger Service): { consentId, userId, type: 'data_processing_notice'|'terms_and_conditions'|'marketing_notifications'|'biometric_liveness', version (doc version hash so we know EXACTLY which legal text was agreed), granted: boolean, locale:'sq'|'en', timestamp, ipHash, deviceId, source:'onboarding'. Immutable; consent withdrawal is a NEW entry, never a mutation — required for Albanian Law 9887 / GDPR-aligned auditability.

VerificationStatus / step-state machine (new, in domain/kyc/): enumerated transitions + a per-step record { step, state:'idle'|'pending'|'passed'|'failed', attempts, lastAttemptAt, lockedUntil } powering OTP rate-limiting and resumability. Pure/testable like the existing engine.ts; the existing engine.check.ts test pattern applies.
OtpChallenge (new, OTP/Verification Service, ephemeral/TTL): { challengeId, channel:'sms'|'email', target, codeHash, expiresAt, attempts, maxAttempts } — code itself never stored in plaintext.

Extend existing store State (src/game/store.ts) with onboarding/KYC fields mirroring the persisted shape: kycStatus, accountOpened, consents summary, and link the existing seed mission 'ob-verify' (game/data.ts) to fire its claim when kycStatus becomes 'verified'. Persist only non-sensitive resume state via partialize (as already done); NEVER persist OTP codes, document images, or banking tokens to AsyncStorage.

AccountApplication / Account (new, User Service): { accountId, userId, iban (AL IBAN), productType:'youth', currency:'ALL'|'EUR', openedAt, asdInsured: true } — surfaces the ASD deposit-insurance and Bank of Albania supervision disclaimers required for a real AL bank account.
security: Two-token / step-up auth: short-lived scope-limited onboarding JWT during the flow (no banking scopes); OAuth2 token-exchange to a full banking session ONLY after kycStatus=verified + account opened — an unverified user can never hold a banking token. Refresh tokens in OS Keychain/Keystore (expo-secure-store), never AsyncStorage.

OTP hardening (fraud + abuse): codes stored only as hashes with short TTL; server-side per-target rate limiting, attempt caps, and lockout (lockedUntil in the step-state machine); resend throttling. Prevents SMS-pumping and brute force on phone/email verification.

KYC document + liveness security: ID images/video go straight to encrypted object storage via short-lived signed URLs, never through app-persisted storage; automated liveness (no human, matching the observed flow) binds a real present human to the identity to stop deepfake/photo-of-photo and referral farming; OCR results require user confirmation (PUT /personal-data) before account open.
Encryption: TLS 1.2+ in transit on every BFF/microservice hop; PII + KYC at rest encrypted, with field-level encryption for high-sensitivity identifiers (national ID number, date of birth).

Consent correctness + Albanian Law 9887 / GDPR-aligned auditability: every consent is an immutable, versioned ledger entry tied to the exact legal-text version, locale, timestamp and device; the data-processing 'Information notice' checkbox and the 'Why do we need it?' plain-language rationale are captured before the corresponding data is collected (purpose limitation / lawful basis). Withdrawal = new entry, never a delete.

RBAC + least privilege: customer scope unlocks only post-verification; KYC review, consent ledger, and PII access are separate back-office roles; the BFF exposes only the per-screen DTO the client needs (no over-fetching of PII to the device).

Referral/rewards fraud protection (brief §7 explicit): inviter attribution and bonus payout validated SERVER-SIDE in the Referral Service at account-open time (the client store's simulateReferral()/coin grants are demo-only and must never be trusted); device-attestation + one-identity-per-liveness binding caps multi-account abuse; idempotent /account endpoint prevents duplicate-account creation.
Eligibility gate: age 18-25 enforced server-side from KYC date-of-birth (not a client claim) — youth-only product; under-18 / over-25 rejected before account open.

Prototype honesty: all disclaimers/consent texts are drafted for a real bank (Raiffeisen Bank Sh.a., supervised by Bank of Albania, deposits insured by ASD) but are clearly a hackathon prototype requiring final legal/compliance review before production — surface this in the legal screens.
